nameMeanings.name: Dawson
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English: patronymic meaning ‘son of Dawe’, from the Middle English personal name Daw(e) (see Daw 1 and 2), which is sometimes a pet form of David (see 2 below) but more generally of Rauf or Raw (i.e. Ralph ), of which Daw(e) is a rhyming form. Alternatively, the patronymic could mean ‘son of Daud(e)’, another Middle English pet form of Ralph evidently used interchangeably with Dawe. Dawson is one of the most numerous patronymics.
Scottish: patronymic, ‘son of Dawe’. In Scotland Dawe is a diminutive of David (compare 1 above).

variants.name: Daw
meanings: English: from Middle English Daw, a rhyming pet form of Raw, a shortened form of Rauf; see Ralph , Rawe , Rawson , and compare Dawkins and Dawson .

English: possibly also from Daw, a pet form of David . This personal name was not especially common in medieval England (see Davey ), but occurred more often in the southwest of England and along the Welsh Marches. Compare Dakin .

English: nickname from Middle English dawe ‘jackdaw’, a bird noted for its sleek black color, raucous voice, and thievish nature, any of which characteristics could readily have given rise to a nickname.

variants.name: Dayson
meanings: patronymic or metronymic meaning ‘son of the dairyman or dairywoman’ (from Middle English day(e), dey(e) + son).
variant of Dawson with the original diphthong /au/ simplified to /a:/ in late Middle English and subsequently pronounced /εɪ/. Compare Dakin for Dawkin (see Dawkins ).

nameMeanings.name: Joan
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Contracted form of Old French Jo(h)anne, from Latin Io(h)anna ( see Joanna ). In England this was the usual feminine form of John from the Middle English period onwards and was extremely popular, but in the 16th and 17th centuries it steadily lost ground to Jane . It was strongly revived in the first part of the 20th century, partly under the influence of George Bernard Shaw's play St Joan ( 1923 ), based on the life of Joan of Arc ( 1412–31 ). Claiming to be guided by the voices of the saints, she persuaded the French dauphin to defy the occupying English forces and have himself crowned, and she led the French army that raised the siege of Orleans in 1429 . The following year she was captured by the Burgundians and sold to the English, and a year later she was burned at the stake for witchcraft at the age of 18 or 19. Her story has captured the imagination of many writers, and she is variously portrayed as a national and political hero, a model of apolitical straightforwardness and honesty, and a religious heroine. She was canonized in 1920 . More recent influences have included the American film actress Joan Crawford ( 1908–77 , born Lucille le Sueur ), the British actress Joan Collins ( b. 1933 ), the American comedienne Joan Rivers ( b. 1933 ), and the West Indian pop singer Joan Armatrading ( b. 1950 ).

variants.name: John
meanings: As a boy's name this represents a revival of Middle English Jan, a by‐form of John , or an adoption of the common European form with this spelling. As a girl's name it is a short form of names such as Janet and Janice .

historicEvents.title: Book of Common Prayer
historicEvents.description: Book of Common Prayer, a product of the English Reformation, was published in 1549 for assistance in the administration of the sacraments and other rites and ceremonies of the church according to the use of the Church of England. The book outlined morning, evening, and communion prayers and orders for baptism and marriage, thus making England a truly Protestant state.

historicEvents.title: Act of Uniformity
historicEvents.description: The Act of Uniformity was passed by the Parliament of England and required all people to go to church once a week. The consequence of not attending church was a fine of 12 pence, which was a considerable amount for a poor person.

historicEvents.title: The First English Colony is Established in America
historicEvents.description: England established its first colony in North America in 1585 as a way to generate additional wealth. The colony was named Virginia, after the virgin Queen Elizabeth I.

historicEvents.title: Elizabeth I Signs Death Warrant for Mary, Queen of Scots
historicEvents.description: Elizabeth I signed the death warrant for Mary, Queen of Scots, on February 1, 1587, as she was found guilty of plotting to assasinate Elizabeth. Mary, Queen of Scots, was beheaded at Fotheringhay Castle in England on February 8, 1587.
historicEvents.urlTitle: Elizabeth I.org - Mary Queen of Scots

en.lifeSummary: Joan Dawson married Thomas Greene on 9 December 1563, in Nottinghamshire, England, United Kingdom. They were the parents of at least 3 sons and 4 daughters. She was buried in Gedling, Nottinghamshire, England.

fr.lifeSummary: Joan Dawson a épousé Thomas Greene le 9 décembre 1563, à Nottinghamshire, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni. Ils ont eu au moins 3 fils et 4 filles. Elleenterrée à Gedling, Nottinghamshire, Angleterre.

de.lifeSummary: Joan Dawson hat am 9. Dezember 1563 in Nottinghamshire, England, United Kingdom Thomas Greene geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 3 Söhne und 4 Töchter. Sie wurde in Gedling, Nottinghamshire, England begraben.

it.lifeSummary: Joan Dawson lei sposato Thomas Greene il 9 dicembre 1563, nel Nottinghamshire, Inghilterra, Regno Unito. Erano genitori di almeno 3 figli e 4 figlie. Fu sepolta a Gedling, Nottinghamshire, England.

ja.lifeSummary: Joan Dawsonは1563年12月9日にイギリスイギリスノッティンガムシュアイアでThomas Greeneと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも3人の息子と4人の娘の親であった。彼女はイギリスノッティンガムシュアイアジェドリングにて埋葬された。

ko.lifeSummary: Joan Dawson(은)는 1563년 12월 9일 영국 잉글랜드 노틴그함실르에서 Thomas Greene(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 세 명의 아들과 네 명의 딸을 두었다. 그녀는 영국 노틴그함실르 게들링에 묻혔다.

pt.lifeSummary: Joan Dawson casou-se com Thomas Greene em 9 de dezembro de 1563, em Nottinghamshire, Inglaterra, United Kingdom. Eles tiveram pelo menos 3 filhos e 4 filhas. Ela foi sepultada em Gedling, Nottinghamshire, Inglaterra.

ru.lifeSummary: Joan Dawson вышла замуж за человека по имени Thomas Greene 9 декабря 1563 г. в местности ноттингхамшир, Англия, Великобритания. У них было по крайней мере 3 сына и 4 дочери. Она была похоронена в местности джедлинг, ноттингхамшир, Англия.

es.lifeSummary: Joan Dawson se casó con Thomas Greene el 9 de diciembre de 1563, en Nottinghamshire, Inglaterra, Reino Unido. Fueron padres de por lo menos 3 hijos y 4 hijas. Fue sepultada en Gedling, Nottinghamshire, Inglaterra.
